---
title: "R Notebook"
output: html_notebook
---

```{r}
library(brms)
library(tidyverse)
library(BayesianId)

data(mtcars)
data("epilepsy")
```
https://github.com/paul-buerkner/brms/issues/219
See the second example in the documentation for "stanvar".
```{r}
formula = count ~ Trt + zBase

# This is based on the second example in the help file for "stanvar".
intercept_prior = set_prior("", class = "Intercept")
b_prior = set_prior("multi_student_t(1, rep_vector(0.0, Kc), r * sigma^2 * V)", class = "b")
sigma_prior = set_prior("gamma(0.01, 0.01)", class = "sigma") # mimic a Jefferey's prior
our_prior = intercept_prior + b_prior + sigma_prior

stan_code = brms::stancode(object = formula,
                             data = epilepsy,
                             prior = our_prior,
                             stanvars = stanvar(name = "V", scode = "matrix[Kc, Kc] V = inverse(Xc'*Xc);", block = "tdata", position = "end") + stanvar(r, name = "r", scode = "real<lower=0> r;", block = "data")
)

print(stan_code)
#compiled_model = cmdstanr::cmdstan_model(cmdstanr::write_stan_file(stan_code))
```

```{r}
r = 0.5
#family = gaussian()
#family = student()
#family = lognormal()
family = bernoulli()
print(names(family))
print(family$dpars)
#family %in% c(gaussian, student, skew_normal, lognormal, gen_extreme_value)
```

```{r}

fit = brm(count ~ Trt + zBase, 
         data = epilepsy,
         prior = set_prior("multi_student_t(1, rep_vector(0.0, Kc), r * sigma^2 * V)", class = "b"),
         stanvars = stanvar(name = "V", scode = "matrix[Kc, Kc] V = inverse(Xc'*Xc);", block = "tdata", position = "end") + stanvar(r, name = "r", scode = "real<lower=0> r;", block = "data"))

summary(fit)
```

```{r}
library(BayesianId)

fit = fit_model(count ~ Trt + zBase, data = epilepsy, r = 0.5)

summary(fit)
```

```{r}
plot(fit)
```


```{r}
fit = brm(mpg ~ cyl + gear, 
                data = mtcars, 
                prior = set_prior("cauchy(0, 0.71*sigma)", class = "b"),
                iter = 11000,
                warmup = 1000,
                chains = 4, # number of chains
                cores = 2,
                refresh = 0, # don't print those annoying iteration updates
                seed = 1212
                )
```

```{r}
rmodels = restricted_models(fit$formula)
```

```{r}
names(rmodels)
```

```{r}
rmodels$included
```

```{r}
rmodels$omitted
```

```{r}
rmodels$included_table
```

```{r}
model_list = rmodels

model_probs = rep(0.0, times = model_list$n_models)
names(model_probs) = model_list$formula_strings
model_probs["count ~ Trt + zBase"] = 0.5
model_probs["count ~ Trt"] = 0.0
model_probs["count ~ zBase"] = 0.2
model_probs["count ~ 1"] = 0.3
print(model_probs)
```

```{r}
par_probs = rep(0.0, times = model_list$n_fixed)
names(par_probs) = model_list$fixed_names

for(i in 1:model_list$n_fixed){
  term = model_list$fixed_names[i]
  includes_term = model_list$included_table[names(model_probs), term]
  par_probs[i] = sum(model_probs[includes_term])
}
print(par_probs)
```

```{r}
term = rmodels$fixed_names[1]
print(term)
includes_term = rmodels$included_table[,term]
print(includes_term)
```



```{r}
bfs = restricted_model_bfs_sdr(fit)
```
```{r}
bfs$model_table %>% round(2)
```

### DOUBLE CHECK CALCULATIONS

```{r}
bfs$incl_table  %>% round(2)
```



```{r}
bf_check = BayesFactor::regressionBF(mpg ~ cyl, data = mtcars)
bf_check/bf_check[3]
```
